summary: In this very brief post, Joel Spolsky highlights Jim Highsmith's 'design the box' technique for product vision. The idea is that teams should imagine designing the retail box for their product as a way to clarify what the product is really about. Joel endorses this as a great approach to figuring out the big picture of the products you build.
key_points: The 'design the box' exercise forces teams to distill their product's value proposition into what would fit on a retail package
Joel credits Jim Highsmith as the source of this product vision technique
The post emphasizes the importance of having a clear big-picture view of the products you build
money_quotes: Jim Highsmith has a great idea for figuring out the big picture of the products you build: 'design the box.'
key_insight: Imagining how you would design the retail box for your product is a powerful exercise for clarifying its core value proposition and big-picture vision.
